criteria_verification.criterion_text: `internal_docs.always_include` paths are prepended to the flag list
criteria_verification.evidence: context_resolver.py lines 172-187 read internal_docs.always_include from origin manifest and validate each path; line 291 starts the ordered_paths list with always_include_paths BEFORE the category-filtered docs are appended (lines 292-293). always_include paths bypass the category filter check entirely (no filter is applied in that loop). criteria_verification.criterion_text: Cycle detection: a manifest already visited in the current chain is not
criteria_verification.evidence: context_resolver.py line 197 initialises the BFS chain as frozenset({origin_manifest_path}); line 283 extends the chain on each recursion (chain | {dep_manifest_path}); lines 257-272 emit GuardKitWarning(code='context_manifest_cycle_detected', details={'reason': 'cycle', ...}) and skip recursion when dep_manifest_path is already in the current chain. Per-chain (not global) tracking allows diamond imports to remain legal. Verified by TestCycleDetection::test_two_node_cycle_resolves_safely (PASSED).

criteria_verification.criterion_text: Documents whose resolved absolute path is outside `read_allowlist` are
criteria_verification.evidence: context_resolver.py lines 372-386 (_validate_doc_path): after the repo-root check, iterates read_allowlist with `_is_within(resolved, allowed)` — if no entry matches, emits GuardKitWarning(code='context_manifest_path_outside_allowlist', message=..., details={'path': abs_path, 'allowlist': [...]}) and returns (False, abs_path) so the doc is omitted from output. Verified by TestReadAllowlistExclusion::test_doc_outside_allowlist_is_omitted_and_warned (PASSED).

criteria_verification.criterion_text: Documents whose path resolves outside the repo root are omitted with a
criteria_verification.evidence: context_resolver.py lines 356-370 (_validate_doc_path): performs `_is_within(resolved, owning_repo_root)` BEFORE the allowlist check; if the resolved path escapes the repo (e.g. via `..` or symlink), emits GuardKitWarning(code='context_manifest_path_outside_repo', details={'path': abs_path, 'repo_root': str(owning_repo_root)}) and returns (False, abs_path). Verified by TestDocPathEscapesRepoRoot::test_path_with_dotdot_escape_is_rejected (PASSED).

criteria_verification.criterion_text: Resolution is **stateless** — two concurrent calls against the same
criteria_verification.evidence: context_resolver.py uses no @functools.cache, no module-level mutable state, and ResolvedContext fields use field(default_factory=list) so every call constructs fresh lists. Each invocation re-reads manifests from disk (lines 152-153, 273). Verified by TestStatelessness::test_two_calls_return_independent_lists which mutates the first result's lists and asserts the second call's lists are independent (PASSED).
criteria_verification.notes: context_resolver.py uses no @functools.cache, no module-level mutable state, and ResolvedContext fields use field(default_factory=list) so every call constructs fresh lists. Each invocation re-reads manifests from disk (lines 152-153, 273). Verified by TestStatelessness::test_two_calls_return_independent_lists which mutates the first result's lists and asserts the second call's lists are independent (PASSED).

criteria_verification.criterion_text: Order is stable: `always_include` first, then categories in
criteria_verification.evidence: context_resolver.py lines 66-72 declare _CATEGORY_ORDER=('architecture', 'decisions', 'specs', 'contracts', 'source') — first-appearance order across DDR-005 dict values. Lines 291-293 assemble ordered_paths by extending always_include_paths first, then iterating _CATEGORY_ORDER and extending docs_by_category[category] (which preserves BFS+declaration order via list.append in the BFS loop at line 234). Verified by TestStableOrder::test_always_include_then_categories_then_declaration_order with mixed-category fixtures (PASSED).

criteria_verification.criterion_text: Symlinks are followed before allowlist check
criteria_verification.evidence: context_resolver.py line 353 (_validate_doc_path) calls `candidate.resolve(strict=False)` BEFORE either the repo-root or allowlist checks. Path.resolve() follows symlinks, so a symlink whose target is outside the allowlist is correctly rejected by the subsequent `_is_within` check. Verified by TestSymlinksFollowedBeforeAllowlistCheck::test_symlink_target_outside_allowlist_is_rejected (PASSED on Linux; skipped on Windows where symlinks require admin).
